seoTitle: Henderson vs Henty — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Henderson and Henty. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Henderson and Henty.
heading: Henderson vs Henty — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Henderson, Western Australia and Henty, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Henderson has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Henty (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Henderson or Henty safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Henderson has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Henty scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Henderson scores 20/100 for bushfire risk. Henty scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Henderson or Henty?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Henderson has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
